scenario_ids: features/plan_generation.feature:8::Every actionable step names an accountable owner
features/plan_generation.feature:38::The plan exports to a shareable artifact
capabilities.summary: Owners, dependencies, milestones, and export are all covered by passing scenarios.
capabilities.title: Plan generation acceptance suite

scenario_ids: features/remote_scope.feature:8::A narrow-population result is reported with its scope
features/remote_scope.feature:18::Transfer to interdependent roles is flagged as unwarranted
features/remote_scope.feature:28::A scope-adjusted estimate is computed from the population
capabilities.summary: Given a narrow-population result, the tool computes scope-adjusted estimates and flags transfer risk.
capabilities.title: Flag unsupported generalization of a scoped effect

scenario_ids: features/stable_tree.feature:8::A finding attaches to a persistent node identifier
features/stable_tree.feature:18::Findings survive a reorganization of the question tree
capabilities.summary: Findings are stored against persistent node identifiers; retrieval across reorganizations is not yet built.
capabilities.title: Stable question-tree acceptance suite

evidence.summary: Participants rated the process as fair, but the small, self-selected sample cannot decide legitimacy at scale.
evidence.title: Internal deliberation pilot

evidence.summary: A preregistered field experiment measures a productivity gain in a narrow call-centre setting.
evidence.title: Ctrip field experiment

evidence.summary: A preregistered synthesis finds the evidence base does not warrant a universal remote-work effect.
evidence.title: Cross-role transfer review

evidence.summary: The prototype stores findings against persistent node identifiers on a synthetic corpus.
evidence.title: Stable-address mechanism

consequences: Fewer missed ownership handoffs than ordinary planning
Shorter time to resolve cross-team dependencies
defeaters: No coordination difference under a representative comparison
Any observed improvement disappears when facilitation is held constant
hypotheses.scope: Cross-functional software teams using the current plan generator
hypotheses.statement: Teams using generated intervention plans miss fewer coordination handoffs than teams using ordinary planning.
hypotheses.subject: ltp.claim:CLM-7
hypotheses.summary: The plan generator's capability is fully implemented; no comparative coordination result is linked yet.
hypotheses.title: Generated intervention plans improve cross-team coordination

consequences: Effect size varies materially by task interdependence
The original gain weakens outside solo, measurable work
defeaters: Comparable gains reproduce across interdependent roles
Role and task structure do not moderate the result
hypotheses.scope: Transfer from solo call-centre work to remote work generally
hypotheses.statement: A productivity gain measured in solo, highly measurable work does not transfer to interdependent roles.
hypotheses.subject: ltp.claim:CLM-1
hypotheses.summary: A qualified, preregistered cross-role synthesis supports the non-generalization claim at comparative-pilot strength.
hypotheses.title: A local productivity gain does not generalize across roles

consequences: New findings attach to existing questions rather than forming isolated claims
Contradictions become visible at the question node
defeaters: Stable addresses do not improve evidence retrieval or comparison
The tree preserves structure while hiding disagreement
hypotheses.scope: Research notes mapped to stable question-node addresses
hypotheses.statement: Mapping findings to stable question-node addresses lets evidence accumulate and contradictions surface at the node.
hypotheses.subject: ltp.entity:IO-1
hypotheses.summary: The stable-address mechanism is demonstrated on fixtures; the accumulation claim has not been tested.
hypotheses.title: A stable question tree lets evidence accumulate

consequences: Perceived legitimacy remains stable as membership expands
Disadvantaged groups retain practical influence over decisions
defeaters: Formal participation exists without practical influence
hypotheses.statement: A cross-border institution can preserve perceived and practical legitimacy as its membership scales.
hypotheses.subject: norms.promise:/governance/_legitimate_at_scale
hypotheses.summary: A preregistered internal pilot returned inconclusive; no capability is claimed for the scaling mechanism.
hypotheses.title: A new governance institution can remain legitimate at global scale

limitations.text: The deliberation pilot was small and self-selected; its inconclusive verdict cannot yet be lifted without a broader cohort.

portfolio.thesis: Keep each proposition's capability, evidence maturity, and scientific conclusion as three honest, independent dimensions.
portfolio.title: Remote-work evidence review

studies.criterion: Precommitted handoff-miss and dependency-resolution-time thresholds across a representative set of teams.
studies.title: Comparative coordination pilot

studies.criterion: Representation, contestability, and practical influence measured against a non-self-selected comparison cohort.
studies.title: Legitimacy-at-scale field study
